page: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with x86_64 architecture Intel processors. The system features 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 375.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 11.72 GB of RAM per core.

For accelerated workloads, the server integrates two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined total of 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The combination of dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs and 375.0 GB of RAM makes this configuration suitable for memory-intensive GPU workloads, such as large language model inference, deep learning training, and complex data science tasks. However, the shared CPU allocation model represents a potential performance tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, dedicated host CPU processing. The high density of GPU memory and system RAM relative to the CPU core count positions this server as a specialized resource for GPU-bound operations rather than general-purpose compute.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is an x86_64 Cloud GPU server featuring 32 Intel vCPUs and 32 physical cores under a shared allocation model. The hardware configuration includes 375.0 GB of system memory, yielding 11.72 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 2200 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs, providing a total of 96 GB of VRAM. This high-density memory and GPU profile is designed for memory-intensive parallel computing tasks, including machine learning, large-scale rendering, and data science workloads. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for tasks requiring dedicated host CPU execution, making this instance best suited for workloads that offload primary processing to the dual GPU accelerators. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address, offering a balanced platform for network-accessible GPU-accelerated applications.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is an x86_64 Cloud GPU server featuring 32 Intel vCPUs, 375 GB of system RAM, a 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and two NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs delivering a combined 96 GB of VRAM.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server with 32 Intel vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, a 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM.
tagline: An accelerated GPU instance featuring dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 96 GB VRAM, and 375 GB RAM for memory-intensive parallel workloads.

32 Intel vCPUs and 32 physical cores with shared CPU allocation
375.0 GB of system memory providing 11.72 GB per core
2200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
One complimentary public IPv4 address included
Optimized for machine learning, data science, and GPU-bound workloads
